reasoning:
The question asks if there will be more than 10 times the baseline number of 'Strategic developments' in New Caledonia during specific 30-day windows. The baseline reference value on 2025-10-16 is 1.083. Given the formula (1 + [30-day average over 360 days] = 1.083), the 30-day average is 0.083. This dictates that exactly ONE event was coded as a 'Strategic development' by ACLED in New Caledonia over the entire preceding 360-day period (1 event / 12 months = ~0.083). For this question to resolve positively, the number of 'Strategic developments' in a single 30-day window must exceed 10.83, meaning at least 11 events must occur.

This threshold requires an unprecedented, roughly 132-fold increase in the rate of 'Strategic developments'. Even during a highly volatile year (October 2024 to October 2025) featuring significant unrest, the Bougival Accord, high-level political talks, arrests, and rejections of agreements, ACLED only coded 1 event as a 'Strategic development' in New Caledonia (likely grouping events or classifying violence strictly under Riots/Protests). It is astronomically unlikely that 11 distinct 'Strategic developments' will be coded within a single 30-day period over the forecasted dates. The probabilities are kept near the absolute floor (0.05) permitted by the constraints, increasing slightly for more distant dates to account for the theoretical possibility of unforeseeable massive regime shifts, sweeping daily arrests, or ACLED methodology changes.
